title: ON VEHICLE SERVICE — Inhibitor Switch

figures_detected.description: View of A/T assembly showing cable, select linkage, hole of manual shaft, hole of inhibitor switch, inhibitor switch, and manual shaft
Close-up view of inhibitor switch showing alignment hole and bolts used during adjustment procedure

steps.text: Remove the snap pin, plain washer and conical washer from the A/T assembly select linkage. Disconnect the control linkage.
Set the manual shaft of the A/T, to the "N" position.
notes: The "N" position is the location where the inhibitor switch adjustment hole and manual shaft adjustment hole are aligned.

steps.text: Loosen the three inhibitor switch bolts.
Insert pin (4 mm diameter) into adjustment holes in both inhibitor switch and manual shaft of A/T assembly.
Tighten the three bolts to the specified torque.
Install the parts that were removed.
Check continuity of the inhibitor switch. (Refer to "Trouble Diagnosis", Electrical Component Inspection.)

content_blocks.description: A/T assembly select linkage area showing inhibitor switch and manual shaft alignment holes
Close-up of inhibitor switch showing alignment hole and bolt locations

tools_referenced.name: Pin (4 mm diameter)
tools_referenced.usage: Insert into adjustment holes in both inhibitor switch and manual shaft of A/T assembly during alignment

model_notes: Procedure applies to A/T (Automatic Transmission) equipped vehicles
